perturbation.original_text: Fox Mobile Entertainment, Inc. ("Fox"), as Administrator for Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation ("Trademark Licensor") and Glu Mobile, Inc. f/k/a Sorrent, Inc. ("Licensee").
perturbation.changed_text: Fox Mobile Entertainment, Inc. ("Fox"), as Administrator for Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation ("Content Owner") and Glu Mobile, Inc. f/k/a Sorrent, Inc. ("Licensee").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Trademark Licensor' to 'Content Owner' creates ambiguity. The Agreement later uses 'Trademark Licensor' which now contradicts the earlier definition. It introduces uncertainty about the role of Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation and its rights regarding the licensed content.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fox Mobile Entertainment, Inc. ("Fox"), as Administrator for Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation ("Content Owner") and Glu Mobile, Inc. f/k/a Sorrent, Inc. ("Licensee"). The parties agree to modify the Agreement as follows: A. "ICE AGE 2" a/k/a "ICE AGE: THE MELTDOWN" AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS: 1. Grant of Rights: Fox grants to Licensee a limited, non-exclusive right and license to distribute the following Wireless Products in connection with the Property "ICE AGE: THE MELTDOWN" in the United States: (A) 2 Scrat voicetones; (B) 2 John Leguizamo voicetones; and (C) 3 premium videos. Fox also grants Licensee a limited, non-exclusive right and license to distribute the following Wireless Products in connection with the Property "ICE AGE 2" outside of the United States: (A) 4 Scrat voicetones; (B) 1 John Leguizamo voicetone; and (C) 3 premium videos. (collectively, "IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products") B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Trademark Licensor' to 'Content Owner' creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' refers to both 'Content Owner', which is the updated term, and 'Trademark Licensor', which is the original term but no longer consistently defined as such. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, introduces ambiguity and potential confusion regarding the rights and responsibilities of Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction is particularly impactful since both snippets are located at the same location.

perturbation.original_text: Grant of Rights: Fox grants to Licensee a limited, non-exclusive right and license to distribute the following Wireless Products in connection with the Property "ICE AGE: THE MELTDOWN" in the United States: (A) 2 Scrat voicetones; (B) 2 John Leguizamo voicetones; and (C) 3 premium videos.
perturbation.changed_text: Grant of Rights: Fox grants to Licensee a limited, non-exclusive right and license to use the following Wireless Products in connection with the Property "ICE AGE: THE MELTDOWN" in the United States: (A) 2 Scrat voicetones; (B) 2 John Leguizamo voicetones; and (C) 3 premium videos.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'distribute' to 'use' significantly alters the scope of the granted rights. 'Distribute' implies the right to sell or provide the products to others, while 'use' is much narrower, potentially limiting the Licensee's ability to monetize the Wireless Products. This creates a contradiction with the royalty clause that assumes distribution and sale, implying that 'use' incorporates 'distribution' which is unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in the following amount: (a) From the first unit sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts (as defined in the Agreement) from Licensee's sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted the right to 'distribute' wireless products, aligning with the Royalty clause which specifies royalties from 'sale and distribution'. Changing 'distribute' to 'use' contradicts the Royalty clause because the royalty is based on 'sale and distribution', implying a right to monetize that the 'use' clause now potentially restricts. This creates an in-text contradiction because the royalty structure assumes a broader right (distribution) than is now granted (use), and the changed term makes it unclear whether 'use' incorporates 'distribution' for royalty purposes.

perturbation.original_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be treated in the same manner as set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be considered full payment. The provisions set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement do not apply.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that VGSL and CSP payments are treated in the same manner as Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement, implying those paragraphs are applicable and further define the payment process. The changed text states that VGSL and CSP payments are full payment. The provisions set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement do not apply, creating a direct contradiction that is intended to serve as the final settlement for all IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VGSL and other CSP Accounting: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be considered full payment. The provisions set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement do not apply. ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 3. No Direct-to-Consumer Distribution Royalty: Licensee shall not be entitled to receive a share of any revenues and shall have no claim to any revenues earned and collected by Fox, or a third-party Fox designates, for the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products which Fox distributes through its and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that VGSL and CSP payments are considered full payment and that provisions in paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) do not apply. The contradicted text essentially repeats this same statement verbatim. Therefore, there is no contradiction introduced by the change, as both sections now align.
